title: 2023 Census: High Level Design | Stats NZ
content:
## 2023 Census: High Level Design
05 October 2021, 1:00pm
The 2023 Census: High Level Design document sets out the priorities for the 2023 Census, scope of what will be developed for the 2023 Census, and how we are responding to what was learned through the 2018 Census.
It includes the features, characteristics, assumptions, and scope statements for the 2023 Census that census teams are working to as they make detailed design decisions and undertake development work for the 2023 Census.
Census design evolves over the census cycle. Refinements, and sometimes changes, may happen because of changes in the environment, feedback from stakeholders, what is learned through operational testing, and because we are committed to continuous improvement in our planning and operations throughout the census cycle. Keep referring to Design of the 2023 Census for the latest information about what the next census will include.
Our mission is to rebuild trust and confidence in census and deliver quality data to our customers. We will do this by:
- driving high response rates
- delivering quality data that meets customer needs
- being transparent and proactive about our approach and our progress
- collaborating with iwi and Māori and helping build their capacity and capability with data.
Higher response rates will help us produce census data that both represents Aotearoa New Zealand and local communities and meets output quality needs.
Additional funding received for the 2023 Census will be focused at raising responses rates for groups in the population that have historically faced barriers to participation. We will invest in solutions to overcome barriers, motivate people to participate, and make it easier to complete census forms.
We are also embedding a te ao Māori lens in our work to ensure we better meet the needs of Māori and iwi. Our Treaty of Waitangi statement reflects our commitment to Māori and iwi as Treaty partners.
The 2023 Census is planned to be held in early March 2023. Census day, around which all census operations are organised, will be confirmed in 2022.
Stats NZ has been funded to deliver the successful parts of the 2018 Census, with extra funding to fix what did not work in 2018 and to deliver the next census to a bigger and more diverse population. This means people will see changes in the 2023 Census, but will also see a lot that is familiar because it has worked well in previous censuses.
The key features and shifts planned for the 2023 Census are:
- more, earlier, and ongoing community engagement activity, including supporting communities to drive their own response to the census through Community Counts initiatives
- more census collectors at census time and census collections will start earlier
- people will have the option of doing the census online or on paper forms, with up to 50 percent of households receiving paper forms
- increased assistance to complete the census, including help at your doorstep in some areas and engagement centres in areas where they are needed most
- questions about sexual identity and gender included for the first time in a census
- increased focus on accessibility, with different formats supporting a wider range of people in Aotearoa New Zealand
- increased focus on building data capability with communities, Māori and iwi, because we know that understanding the value of census data and how to use it is a strong motivator to participation
- using a combined methodology, which involves collecting responses using census forms and using administrative data (other data sources) – while used for the 2018 Census to address data quality issues, a combined method model is an intentional part of the design of the 2023 Census to maximise the quality of census data
- planning for limited change to the census form and the products and services we will provide post-census (we will validate what was intended for the 2018 Census with partners, customers, and stakeholders before finalising for the 2023 Census).
